content:
IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.

If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill using the Skill tool BEFORE any response, any action, any clarifying question.

User instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" does not mean skip workflows. Check for skills FIRST.

## Priority Chain

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md contradicts a skill, follow CLAUDE.md. The user is in control.

## Anti-Rationalization

These thoughts mean STOP — you are rationalizing skipping a skill check:

| Thought                              | Reality                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| "This is simple enough"              | Simple tasks have skills too. Check.                                           |
| "I already know how"                 | Skills contain project-specific overrides you don't have memorized. Invoke it. |
| "Let me explore first"               | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first.                                   |
| "I need more context"                | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. Always.                         |
| "The skill is overkill"              | Simple things become complex. Use it.                                          |
| "I'll just do this one thing first"  | Check BEFORE doing anything. No exceptions.                                    |
| "I remember this skill"              | Skills evolve. Read the current version via the Skill tool.                    |
| "This doesn't need a formal process" | If a skill exists for it, use it. Period.                                      |
| "Let me look at the code quickly"    | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills.                             |
| "This feels productive"              | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this.                         |
| "This is just a discussion"          | Questions about features, bugs, or architecture ARE development tasks. Check.  |

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, commit format, verification): Follow exactly. Do not adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (React patterns, styling, architecture): Adapt principles to context. The spirit matters more than the letter.

The skill itself tells you which type it is. When unclear, treat as rigid.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorm, start, planning, debugging) — these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Domain skills second** (react, typescript, tanstack) — these guide execution
3. **Workflow skills third** (commit, verification, close) — these govern shipping

## Subagent Exemption

If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific, scoped task — skip this enforcement. Subagents follow their prompt, not the full skill ceremony.

import { createFileRoute } from "@tanstack/react-router"; import { Badge, Stack, Typo } from "@imfusion/web-ui"; import { Layout } from "../../components/layout"; import { Doc } from "../../components/doc"; export const Route = createFileRoute("/components/typo")({ component: TypoPage }); function TypoPage() { return ( Primitive Typo Typographic primitives for headings, paragraphs, and inline text. Each member maps to a fixed semantic element and consumes the typography token vocabulary; pick the component that matches the meaning of the text, not the size you happen to want. Preview Display heading — H1 Section heading — H2 Subsection — H3 Group — H4 A lead paragraph introduces a section with slightly larger, supportive text. Use it sparingly — one per section. Body paragraph. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Use InlineCode{" "} for short code references inside running text, or Typo.Highlight to draw attention to a phrase. Small text for captions, footnotes, and supporting metadata. "Design adds value faster than it adds costs." — a quote rendered with Typo.Blockquote.
  • Unordered list — Typo.UnorderedList
  • Each item inherits body type
  • Native <li> children
    •
  • Ordered list — Typo.OrderedList
  • Numbered markers
  • Same body type
    •      Usage
  • Pick the component that matches the meaning of the text — heading levels mirror document structure, not visual weight.
  • Margin is 0 on every component. Spacing belongs to the parent layout — flex / grid gaps, stack containers — not to the type itself.
  • The color prop accepts any WebUIVariant:{" "} main, support,{" "} minor, primary,{" "} negative, positive,{" "} warning, info.
  • Headings (H1–H4) accept{" "} id for anchor linking.
  • OrderedList and UnorderedList accept native <li> children — there is no separate{" "} Typo.ListItem.
    • Examples Color variants Main Support Minor Primary Positive Negative Warning Info {`Primary Negative`} Inline code in a paragraph Run npm run dev to start the playground, or{" "} npm run build to produce a publishable bundle in{" "} dist/. {` Run npm run dev to start the playground. `} Highlight inside running text The CSS color-mix() function is used to derive subtle background tints from existing tokens — see Typo.InlineCode and the highlight tint above. {` The CSS color-mix() function ... `} Lead paragraph against body A typography library is the backbone of any design system — it sets the rhythm of every page. The body paragraph carries the bulk of the content. It should feel comfortable to read at length: a measured size, generous line-height, and a calm color. {`... ...`} Blockquote with attribution "The details are not the details. They make the design." — Charles Eames {`... — Charles Eames`} Ordered and unordered lists
  • Disc markers
  • Inherits body sizing
  • Margin between items is intrinsic
    •
  • Numbered markers
  • Use for sequential steps
  • Same vertical rhythm as the unordered variant
    •      {`

content.text:
# AGENTS.md

Repo-level orientation. Per-package guidance lives next to each package — start there for any concrete work:

- [`packages/web-ui/AGENTS.md`](./packages/web-ui/AGENTS.md) — the publishable library
- [`apps/playground/AGENTS.md`](./apps/playground/AGENTS.md) — the consumer-side PoC app

## What this repo is

PoC for `@imfusion/web-ui` (Jira: WEBSDK-136). Demonstrates the _shape_ of a publishable Web UI library without actually publishing. Each evaluated component library lives on its own branch — `main` = Base UI.

## Cross-cutting reading

- [`docs/style.md`](./docs/style.md) — code style shared by both packages
- [`plans/`](./plans/) — short-lived implementation plans driven by the `dev:flow` skill. If a plan and `architecture.md` disagree, architecture wins.
- [`ROADMAP.md`](./ROADMAP.md) — PoC scope, progress, and Nik's dev log. Read the latest LOG entry before starting work.

## Workflow

- `npm run dev` — lib watch + playground in parallel
- `npm run dev:playground` — playground only
- `npm run build` — `@imfusion/web-ui` build (`packages/web-ui/dist/`)
- `npm run typecheck` — `tsc --noEmit` across all workspaces

## Code Intelligence (Optional)

> Requires [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev).

TypeScript LSP is available via the `typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official` plugin. Run `mise install` at the repo root. Use `LSP` operations (`goToDefinition`, `findReferences`, `hover`) in preference to grep/glob for code navigation.

## Don't

- Don't make changes without reading the latest `ROADMAP.md` LOG entry.
- Don't edit the `ROADMAP.md` LOG unless explicitly asked. Nik maintains it.
- Don't expand a plan's scope mid-session without naming it explicitly.
Path constraint must be relative to the workspace: /Users/brunner/repos/imfusion/web-ui-poc/packages/web-ui/src

packages/web-ui/src/index.ts

# AGENTS.md — @imfusion/web-ui

Publishable design-system library. The implementation library is invisible to consumers (see [`docs/architecture.md`](./docs/architecture.md), rule 1).

## Read

- [`docs/prd.md`](./docs/prd.md) — what & why
- [`docs/architecture.md`](./docs/architecture.md) — non-negotiable rules
- [`docs/glossary.md`](./docs/glossary.md) — canonical terms
- [`docs/new-component.md`](./docs/new-component.md) — recipe for a new primitive
- [`docs/style.md`](./docs/style.md) — folder + CSS-class conventions

## Don't

- Don't `extends` an upstream interface or `{...props}` spread in primitives. Map fields by name.
- Don't export a primitive's `Props`. Consumers derive via `React.ComponentProps<typeof X>`.

content.text: # ROADMAP **Legend:** - `[x]` — Done - `[~]` — Partially done / WIP - `[ ]` — Not started ## Scope What this PoC needs to be considered complete. - [x] Monorepo setup - [x] Library package - [x] `Web UI Provider` - [x] Architecture documentation - [x] Design token vocabulary - [x] Basic Playground app - [x] Pre-commit hooks - [ ] Linting accross all packages - [~] Component primitives - [~] Web UI Explorer with Docs (via MDX) and Config and Knob Panel - [~] Design token types (`WebUISize`, `WebUIColor`, etc.) - [ ] User ready docs/ folder - [ ] Config-driven theming - [ ] Storybook - [ ] Hosting of Web UI Explorer ### Component Primitives - [x] Badge - [x] Button - [x] Typo - [x] [Slider · Base UI](https://base-ui.com/react/components/slider) - [ ] [Stack · Mantine UI ](https://github.com/mantinedev/mantine/tree/master/packages/%40mantine/core/src/components/Stack) ## Further Goals Ideas and features beyond the PoC scope. - [ ] Live + code sync for component examples - [ ] Vitest unit tests - [ ] Visual regression tests - [ ] Consumer codegen - [ ] Release flow - [ ] LLM accessibility (CLI `imf-ui`) ## LOG ### Open - [ ] `web-ui`: Think about hook strategy for something like `useDisclosure` (Re-export from other lib?) - [ ] `web-ui`: Export `useDisclosure` hook (app-shell.tsx uses inline useState booleans for nav/knobs open state) - [ ] `web-ui`: Simplify Props interface and extend it also by base html attributes props - [ ] `web-ui`: QUESTION: Is single component packages better than tree-shaking from base UI? - [ ] `web-ui`: Pin dependencies with script - [ ] `web-ui`: Generic media-query hook (e.g. `useMediaQuery(query)` / `useBreakpoint(name)`) — playground responsiveness is broken; AppShell currently uses an inline `matchMedia` listener as a workaround. Replace once the hook exists. ### 2026.05.07 - Thursday - [~] General review of new Playground UI - Nav sidebar as its own component + config; AppShell should be dumb layout only - Install Lucide (or similar) icon library — replace ☰ / ⚙ unicode icons - Extract dashboard page styles into components (DashboardHero, DashboardCard) instead of flat `index.css` - Migrate to CSS Modules for consistency with web-ui (no FOUC risk in pure CSR; deferred) - Generated CSS panel too small — increase `max-height`in`.shell-generated-css` - [ ] `playground`: Rename `playground` to `explorer` - [ ] `web-ui/Typo`: Font-Family property (Mono / Sans) - [ ] `playground`: Replace Space Mono with Space Grotesk - [ ] `playground`: Markdown-driven doc pages via MDX — see [plan](./plans/03-mdx-docs.md) - [ ] `playground`: `config` file (absorb `apps/playground/src/nav-config.ts`) - [ ] `llm`: Add style rules for css modules and compount components #### Knobbies About the intent knobs: - I think we only need one luminosity knob for the intent tokens, but differentated by colorscheme (light/dark). - I imagine the intent knobs feel to have one overarching slider for the luminosity and the chroma, and a hue slider for each intent. - [ ] `web-ui`: Export `useKnobs` hook (or similar) with programmatic token metadata — removes manual string sync in `knob-config.ts` - [ ] `web-ui/knobs`: Add Knobs for Foreground colors (Backgrounds/aka surfaces are already there) - [ ] `web-ui/knobs`: Add Knobs for luminance (see above) - [ ] `playground`: Persist knob values in local storage - [ ] Cleanup deferred to the rework (from impl-review of the playground refactor): - `KnobPanel` still hard-imports `KNOB_CONFIGS` for grouping. `useTokenOverrides` is catalog-as-input; `KnobPanel` should match (accept a `catalog` prop) so `useKnobs()` output flows through end-to-end. ### 2026.05.06 - Wednesday - [x] Split `variables.css` into `variables/` topic files (colors, sizes, borders, typography, easings, layers) - [x] Add `--imf-ui-font-family-*` + semantic aliases (heading/body/ui/code) - [x] Add `--imf-ui-font-weight-*`, `--imf-ui-font-size-*`, `--imf-ui-font-lineheight-*`, `--imf-ui-font-letterspacing-*` - [x] Add `--imf-ui-ease-*` (open-props scale 1–5 + in/out variants) - [x] Add `--imf-ui-layer-*` (z-index 1–5 + important) - [x] Replace custom shadow tokens with open-props shadow system (oklch + color-mix) - [x] Replace `--play-font-*` → `--imf-ui-font-family-*` and `--play-ease` → `--imf-ui-ease-4` across all playground CSS - [x] `web-ui`: Typography component (modelled on `~/repos/nikbrunner/nbr.haus/src/components/Typo/`) - [~] General review of new Playground UI - Nav sidebar as its own component + config;
